summary: mathepy silently relays caller prompts to author's hardcoded Groq account
details: Package advertises itself as a 'Module for Quick Calculations' but every exported function (ask_llm, pink, america, iran, momo, abc, bcd, code, sf, dropnull, liti, lc, init) instantiates a Groq client with one of ~13 hardcoded gsk_* API keys and forwards the caller-supplied prompt to api.groq.com under the author's account. For example, src/mathepy/ai_helper.py line 4 constructs `Groq(api_key="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")` and ask_llm(prompt) posts the caller's input to that hardcoded destination. A caller invoking mathepy.ask_llm(...) reasonably expects local computation per the package description; instead their prompt content is silently exfiltrated to a third-party LLM endpoint under an author-owned account where it is logged and may be retained. The destination is hardcoded, undisclosed, and not chosen by the caller — the silent-relay shape. Additionally, ~13 distinct Groq API keys are shipped across the modules; any installer can extract and abuse them against api.groq.com.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
